brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Yes) with valid reasoning grounded in folk psychology; identifies three distinct mechanisms (norm violation, counterfactual dependence, behavioral asymmetry) and addresses the repairman's framing, showing multi-level causal attribution analysis; proceeds linearly without naming potential biases or checking its own assumptions about typical intuition; performs no verification—no sanity check on whether Claire could equally be named cause, no boundary test; acknowledges the 'technically accurate' repairman framing as an implicit alternative view but treats it as obviously overridden rather than analyzing it structurally; mentions two competing framings (moral vs. technical causation) but does not systematically evaluate when each would dominate or why typical people discount the technical framing; reasoning steps are numbered and traceable but the psychological premises (e.g., norm-violation → causal salience) are asserted rather than derived.
